preamble: The reduction of the velar nasal [ŋ] to the palatal glide [j] (here represented by 'y') is a form of lenition and fronting. The consonant loses its nasal and velar characteristics, becoming a simple approximant.

examples.note: Occurs in various linguistic contexts as a form of reduction.

sources: Ladefoged, P., & Maddieson, I. (1996). The Sounds of the World's Languages.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Philology.
